figures.caption: Figure 1: Overview of the SWE-Shepherd framework. The framework consists of six stages. (1) Training tasks are collected from SWE-Bench. (2) An LLM-based agent tries to solve the tasks by interacting with the codebase and generate solution trajectories composed of intermediate reasoning steps and actions. (3) Each intermediate step is assigned a scalar reward reflecting its contribution toward solving the task. (4) A dataset is constructed where the input text (problem description, execution history, and current action) is paired with the computed reward labels. (5) A Process Reward Model (PRM) is trained by feeding text representations from the LLM into an MLP head and optimizing with mean squared error (MSE) loss to predict step-level rewards. (6) During inference, the trained PRM guides reward-aware search, prioritizing higher-quality intermediate steps to improve problem-solving performance.
Table 1: Performance on SWE-Bench Verified using gpt-5-mini (100 tasks, max 30 steps).
